2606.22911.title: ThermoLLM: Thermodynamics-Aware HVAC Control with Spatial-Semantic Knowledge Graph
2606.22911.paper_summary: Multi-zone HVAC control is a spatial decision problem in which indoor thermal evolution and control decisions depend not only on outdoor conditions and internal heat gains but also on zone layout, physical adjacency, and delayed thermal interactions across the building. Recent LLM-based HVAC controllers have shown that prompt-based control is feasible. However, these methods typically rely on task descriptions, observation values, short textual feedback, or unstructured retrieval, which limits their ability to reason about zone coupling, thermal response, and building dynamics. This paper presents a thermodynamics-aware LLM control framework for a five-zone EnergyPlus building simulation. The controller is grounded in a physics-informed spatial knowledge graph derived from Brick-style building semantics and linked with recent interaction history. At each control step, the model receives the current building state, graph-structured spatial context, and recent environment-controller history, enabling it to make decisions that reflect both building structure and short-term thermal evolution. We evaluate the framework against standard control baselines and several LLM-based alternatives. Results show that the proposed approach achieves the best overall energy-comfort trade-off and the lowest PMV violation while maintaining energy-efficient operation.
2606.22911.paper_summary_zh: 多區域 HVAC 控制是一個空間決策問題，其中室內熱演變和控制決策不僅依賴於戶外條件和內部熱增益，還依賴於區域佈局、物理相鄰性以及整棟建築內的延遲熱互動。最近基於 LLM 的 HVAC 控制器顯示出基於提示的控制是可行的。然而，這些方法通常依賴於任務描述、觀察值、簡短的文本反饋或非結構化檢索，這限制了它們對區域耦合、熱響應和建築動態的推理能力。本文提出了一個熱力學感知的 LLM 控制框架，用於五區域 EnergyPlus 建築模擬。該控制器基於從 Brick 風格建築語義中衍生的物理知識圖，並與最近的互動歷史相連。在每個控制步驟中，模型接收當前建築狀態、圖結構的空間上下文和最近的環境-控制器歷史，使其能夠做出反映建築結構和短期熱演變的決策。我們將該框架與標準控制基準和幾個基於 LLM 的替代方案進行評估。結果顯示，所提出的方法實現了最佳的整體能量-舒適權衡，並在保持能效運行的同時達到了最低的 PMV 違規。
2606.22911.author: Kirtan Bhatt et.al.
2606.22911.authors: Kirtan Bhatt, Xiachong Lin, Matthew Amos, Flora D. Salim, Wen Hu
